varieties.name: Copenhagen Market
varieties.origin: Denmark, 1909 - developed by H. Hartmann & Company
varieties.traits: Round, compact heads 15-20cm diameter, 1.4-1.8kg (3-4 lbs); medium-green to bluish-green outer leaves; white centers
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dense, crisp, sweet, creamy white interior; excellent coleslaw quality
varieties.growing_notes: Widely adapted heirloom; tolerates some heat but must mature before summer; crack-resistant; stores 3-6 months. Plant March-April in humid subtropical climates for winter harvest. Popular in subtropical regions.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Australia), Eden Seeds (QLD), most Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Golden Acre
varieties.traits: Compact plants; round heads 12-18cm diameter, 1-1.5kg; short season variety (65-80 days)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, tender, fine texture; light green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Good production; reliable in various conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Heat-tolerant heirloom suitable for humid subtropical climates; yellowing disease resistant; space-saving (can plant 38cm apart). Thrives in subtropical conditions. Performs well in warm climates.
varieties.availability: Eden Brothers, True Leaf Market, The Seed Collection

varieties.name: Sugarloaf
varieties.traits: Conical/pointed heads; compact growth habit

varieties.name: Red Drumhead
varieties.traits: Large drumhead shape; deep red-purple color; 2-3kg heads

varieties.name: Wombok (Napa/Chinese Cabbage)
varieties.origin: China, 5th century; cultivated in Australia for decades
varieties.traits: Cylindrical/oblong heads 25-50cm long; pale green crinkled leaves; white ribs; 2-3kg
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild, sweet, succulent; crisp white stems; tender pale green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate; can be grown year-round in warm regions; fast-maturing. Heat-tolerant; popular in Asian cuisine. Recommended for tropical/subtropical areas. Good humidity tolerance.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Eden Seeds, most Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Primo
varieties.traits: Round, uniform heads; medium size
varieties.growing_notes: Successfully grown in humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Plant mid-February to mid-March after rain. Transplant one month later. Performs well in Queensland.
varieties.availability: Queensland commercial nurseries

varieties.name: Mini Cabbage
varieties.traits: Small, quick-growing heads to 1.2kg; compact plants; crisp green leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet flavor; good cooked or raw
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for humid subtropical climates gardens with limited space; slow bolting; suitable for subtropical warm humid climate. Ideal for container growing. Plant autumn-winter.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Australia)

varieties.name: Brunswick
varieties.traits: Large drum heads 25cm diameter, 2.7-4kg; dark green outer leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Solid, dense, good flavor; white interior
varieties.growing_notes: Cold hardy but adaptable; can grow spring and fall in humid subtropical climates. Excellent storage (winter keeper). Very versatile heirloom. Plant 45cm apart.
varieties.availability: Burpee, Eden Brothers, international suppliers

varieties.name: Charleston Wakefield
varieties.traits: Compact, dark green conical heads; 1.8-2.7kg; 70-80 days
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable, grown easily in most regions
varieties.growing_notes: Heat-tolerant heirloom suitable for humid subtropical climates. Early maturing. Plant 45-60cm apart. Can grow in Zones 1-10 including subtropical.
varieties.availability: Burpee, Eden Brothers, Mary's Heirloom Seeds

varieties.name: Early Jersey Wakefield

varieties.name: Mammoth Red Rock

varieties.flesh_fruit: Crisp, sweet, mild; purple-red to core

varieties.origin: USA heirloom, 1880s; cross of Flat Dutch × Drumhead

varieties.growing_notes: Excellent heat tolerance - holds well into hot summer months. Perfect for humid subtropical climates climate. Great for sauerkraut, coleslaw, fresh eating. Tolerates hot, dry weather and cool climates.

varieties.name: Late Flat Dutch

varieties.name: Pusa Mukta (SEL.8) 🌏

varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous Indian development; resistant to black rot (Xanthomonas campestris) - major advantage for Tropical monsoon humid climate. Specially bred for disease-prone areas. Best for highland cultivation.

varieties.name: Unnati 🌏

varieties.name: Green Voyager
